paper_title: Human-level control through deep reinforcement learning
passage: A deep Q-network learned policies from pixels and achieved strong scores across a suite of Atari games.
plain_explanation: Using only game images and scores as input, the agent learned actions that performed well on many of the tested Atari games. Results varied across games, so the evidence supports broad benchmark performance rather than universal human-level control.
rewrite_strategy: Translate inputs and feedback, then qualify the headline claim.
faithfulness_notes: Retains the multi-game result while noting variation.
risk_notes: Atari performance does not establish reliability in real-world environments.

passage: PPO optimizes a surrogate objective while limiting how far a policy update moves from the previous policy.
plain_explanation: PPO improves an agent in repeated steps, but discourages any single update from changing its behavior too sharply. This is intended to make training more stable while remaining relatively simple to implement.
rewrite_strategy: Describe the update constraint as a behavioral limit.
risk_notes: Performance and stability still depend on tasks and hyperparameters.
